methods.description: Process for separating mineral fractions by density and preparing samples for geochemical analysis and SEM/LA-ICP-MS.

steps.description: Unconsolidated sand was obtained from the Barrytown deposit.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain unconsolidated sand from Barrytown deposit

steps.description: The ilmenite was sampled from several hundred tonnes of ilmenite separated in 1989.
The heavy and superheavy mineral fractions were taken from about 500 m from the shore about 3 km north of the Barrytown township at a depth ranging from 10-30 m.
steps.searchable_summary: collect heavy and superheavy mineral fractions from specified location

steps.description: For density separation of the heavy fraction of the sand, a wave (shaker) table was used.
steps.searchable_summary: use wave table for density separation of sand

steps.description: The grains were mounted in epoxy resin and polished for use in both SEM and LA-ICP-MS.
steps.searchable_summary: mount and polish grains for SEM and LA-ICP-MS

steps.description: A subsample of the mineral concentrates were dried at 120 °C then calcined at 980 °C, ground then fused with a flux to form standard fused discs for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: dry, calcine, grind, and fuse mineral concentrates to form fused discs

methods.description: Analysis of fused glass discs using LA-ICP-MS to determine elemental composition of mineral fractions.

steps.description: Three mineral fractions were fused and analyzed by LA-ICP-MS.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze fused mineral fractions using LA-ICP-MS

steps.description: The 'ilmenite' fraction contained specific elemental concentrations.
steps.searchable_summary: determine elemental concentrations in ilmenite fraction

steps.description: The heavy and superheavy fractions show a high level of zirconium.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze zirconium levels in heavy and superheavy fractions
